author: Khyati Jindal
review_text: Primary experience : Decent products, Less vareity in fresh produce, just a health filter layer on top any other e-commerce so, i would want it to have a really good filter if that is product straight away, under dibetic it suggest gluten free products. for certain type 2 dibeates , protien products are sugested diet and straight away suggesting gluten ( which is a protien ) , free is crazy will update the experience after a month
sentiment_reason: User appreciates the products but finds the health filter inaccurate, specifically mapping diabetic to gluten-free.
specific_issue: Health filter categorizes gluten-free products under the diabetic filter inaccurately.
feature_request: Better and more accurate health filtering for dietary needs.
churn_reason: User is waiting a month to update experience based on product relevance.
priority_reason: Incorrect health mapping degrades the core value prop of health-conscious shopping.
action_item: Audit and remap health filter tags so 'diabetic' surfaces low-GI/high-protein items rather than defaulting to gluten-free.
key_insight: Inaccurate health-based categorization undermines trust in the platform's 'clean label' branding.

author: narasimhan iyengar
review_text: easy to operate application.
sentiment_reason: User finds the app easy to operate.
priority_reason: Generic positive feedback with no actionable gap.
key_insight: Simple app navigation is a key satisfaction driver.

author: vinay karthik
review_text: fantastic loved the quality, quantity and packaging. delivery agent knew only Hindi, as a non Hindi speaker, it took extra 15 mins for the delivery. training is required for Non Hindi speaking states for delivery.
sentiment_reason: Loves quality and packaging but faced delivery delays due to the agent's language barrier.
specific_issue: Delivery agent only spoke Hindi, causing a 15-minute delay for a non-Hindi speaking customer.
feature_request: Training for delivery agents in non-Hindi speaking states.
priority_reason: Language barriers in last-mile delivery cause friction and SLA breaches.
action_item: Implement local-language requirement checks or basic training modules for last-mile delivery partners in specific regions.
key_insight: Last-mile communication gaps directly impact delivery times in non-Hindi speaking states.

author: Chiru Gowda
review_text: W app, really good mangoes
sentiment_reason: User highly praises the app and the quality of mangoes.
key_insight: Seasonal produce like mangoes acts as a strong positive trigger for customers.

author: Ashish
review_text: They cheat by giving offers of free items on purchase of a certain amount but never delivered it even after raising 2-3 complaints....
sentiment_reason: Promised free items were never delivered despite multiple complaints.
specific_issue: Free promotional items missing from order; CS failed to resolve after 3 complaints.
churn_reason: User feels cheated by unfulfilled marketing promises.
priority_reason: Unfulfilled promises paired with repeated CS failure creates a severe trust break.
action_item: Audit promotional fulfillment logic to ensure free items are hard-linked to the picker manifest, and escalate tickets with >2 contacts.
key_insight: Marketing offers that fail at fulfillment turn potential promoters into active detractors.

author: Anees Haidary
review_text: Ordered milk subscription for first time and milk didn't get delivered. When contacted customer care, I receive a message that my address door number is not mentioned he ce refund processed. I've been ordering from first club for veggies and fruits and it always have been delivered. How come the most essential need of milk not delivered? I want refund of my money and that's also not being processed. I want to cancel my subscription.
sentiment_reason: Milk subscription not delivered, support blamed missing door number, refund not processed.
themes: Subscription | Delivery & Speed | Customer Support | Returns & Refunds
specific_issue: Subscription failed on first order; CS provided poor excuse and failed to process refund.
churn_reason: Explicit intent to cancel subscription due to non-delivery and missing refund.
priority_reason: Money trust break (no refund) + high churn intent (cancelling subscription).
action_item: Add strict address validation (including door number) at checkout for subscriptions, and automate instant refunds for undelivered first-time subscriptions.
key_insight: Failing the first order of a subscription permanently destroys the recurring revenue opportunity.

author: Krishnarao Bidikar
review_text: Authentic and genuine quality products
sentiment_reason: Praises authentic and genuine quality products.
key_insight: Perceived authenticity drives product satisfaction.

author: gokul_chowdhary
review_text: I recently saw Sunpure and saffola oils in your app? really? You are becoming the very thing you swore to destroy.
sentiment_reason: User is disappointed to see mass-market brands (Sunpure, Saffola) on a platform promising clean products.
specific_issue: Mass-market brands listed on the app contradict the core D2C 'clean label' promise.
churn_reason: Brand dilution is making the user question the platform's integrity.
priority_reason: Catalog integrity issue that threatens the app's unique value proposition.
action_item: Audit catalog and implement strict gating criteria to prevent mainstream commercial brands from diluting the premium 'clean label' category.
key_insight: Listing regular mass-market brands damages the trust of users who joined specifically for curated clean products.

author: Siva Kumar Gudivada

author: Himanshu Puri
review_text: on one hand we have cyber crime experts telling people not to click on any links coming in messages, on other hand we have applications like this which are sending referral links to people in messages. this is poor design. 16 may - add credit screen, scan or nfc option fills details. i added cvv and name. but it won't let me pay?? because the expiry date has to be added manually. poor experience again.
sentiment_reason: Complains about spammy SMS referral links and a bug in the credit card payment screen.
specific_issue: Expiry date input in credit card form is broken/manual, preventing successful payment. SMS links are seen as a security risk.
churn_reason: User is blocked from paying due to UI failure.
priority_reason: Checkout blocking bug directly impacts revenue.
action_item: Fix credit card entry form to allow seamless automatic or manual entry of the expiry date without blocking payment submission.
key_insight: Payment form friction not only stops a transaction but frustrates users into leaving negative reviews.

author: Ilamathi Kannan
review_text: Quality and variety of assortment are very good. I started using the app recently and was just becoming a regular. but unfortunately I would prefer to stop now due to very bad customer service experience. I had missing items is the order and in fact I had placed the order the first place precisely for that product. Customer service representative denied replacement within the 1 hr window which is the usual delivery time and also denied reversing the handling and delivery charges.
sentiment_reason: Primary item missing from order, CS denied replacement and refund for handling/delivery charges.
specific_issue: Missing items in order; CS refused replacement within 1 hr and refused to refund delivery/handling fees.
churn_reason: Explicit intent to stop using the app due to bad CS experience.
priority_reason: Money trust break + explicit churn intent over rigid refund policy.
action_item: Update CS tooling and policy to automatically refund delivery and handling charges when core items are missing from an order.
key_insight: Refusing to refund delivery fees when the company fails to deliver the main item instantly destroys user trust.

review_text: have ordered twice from ur app ...happy with the service...please keep it going like this

author: sindhushree prasad
review_text: First two orders were smooth and quality, excellent... fresh vegetables, eco friendly packing... all good. From the third one, it has been an ordeal... one instance was the wrong order sent after waiting for more than two hours... replacing took another two hours. Another order had items missing and rotten onions... needless to say, I'm not impressed.
sentiment_reason: Third order failed severely with wrong items, massive delays, missing items, and rotten produce.
themes: Delivery & Speed | Product Quality | Customer Support
specific_issue: Received wrong order, waited 2 hours for replacement, next order had missing items and rotten onions.
churn_reason: User says 'needless to say, I'm not impressed' after multiple cascading failures.
priority_reason: Major operational QA failure at the dark store level causing cascading churn.
action_item: Implement a mandatory QA checklist at picker stations to prevent wrong-order dispatch and rotten produce inclusion.
key_insight: Even a perfect initial experience is wiped out entirely if subsequent orders suffer severe QA failures.

author: Arunkumar Jayavel

author: Matru S
review_text: poor quality product and went in scamming the customers. Initially tried with big promises on quality, delivery and support.. now it's too pathetic. They don't have proper customer service, the marketing team responds horribly on the WhatsApp group.

sentiment_reason: Experienced order failures, pending refund, and an app update broke their login.
specific_issue: Refund pending for failed order; app update causes 'failed to send code' preventing login.
churn_reason: Cannot log in to access service or check refund status.
priority_reason: Login blocking bug prevents any usage and exacerbates refund anxiety.
action_item: Investigate and resolve the OTP generation failure ('failed to send code') introduced in the latest app update.
key_insight: Releasing auth bugs effectively locks users out of their money and the platform simultaneously.

author: UltraCoolFreakOfNature
review_text: Good experience on my first delivery. They have maintained the promised quality of products. I am glad I found this app. But I am also not getting excited. Only time will tell if they are going to maintain this level, once more customers start ordering on their app and they become well established. I wish the team good luck. I hope I am proven wrong about complacency setting in over time.

priority_reason: No immediate issue, just skepticism about scaling.

Overall, the service feels reliable and thoughtfully done. Wishing the team a strong and successful road ahead!

author: khushi bargoti
review_text: I really liked the app it was really nice but I after my first order the app was unserviceable in my area and my wallet too had like 63+30 I think not sure so neither can I use the app nor get money🙁🫤
sentiment_reason: App became unserviceable after first order, trapping money in the wallet.
specific_issue: User's location became unserviceable, rendering app useless and trapping wallet funds.
churn_reason: User is effectively locked out of the service.
priority_reason: Trapped wallet funds due to operational polygon changes creates a massive trust break and legal risk.
action_item: Add automated wallet refund trigger when a user's saved default address transitions to an unserviceable status.
key_insight: Dynamic geofencing must account for active user wallets to prevent holding customer funds hostage.

author: Mayank Pandey
review_text: I was about to create account but after reading terms and privacy policy, I uninstalled the app. Here are few points: 1: Data may be used/shared as per their internal policies 2: Limited clarity on how securely our data is handled 3: They are not responsible for losses, damages, or outcomes, even if something goes wrong on their platform 4: Everything is tilted in favor of them

action_item: Revise privacy policy terms and conditions UX to highlight data security, fair usage, and user-friendly dispute resolution clauses.
key_insight: Aggressive, legalese-heavy T&Cs scare off high-intent, highly educated users.

author: Shubham Patil

author: harsha chowdary
review_text: ​I am extremely disappointed with the service provided by First Club. I placed an order worth over ₹500, yet there was zero seriousness in fulfilling it. ​The delivery was never initiated, and the rider refused to pick up any of my calls. To make matters worse, after I raised a formal concern, the order was simply canceled without a proper resolution. It is frustrating to see a platform show such a lack of commitment to its customers. ​Trying this app is a complete waste of time.
sentiment_reason: High value order was never delivered, rider ignored calls, and order was cancelled without resolution.
specific_issue: Rider went rogue (no delivery, no calls answered) and CS auto-cancelled the order instead of helping.
churn_reason: Calls the app a 'complete waste of time'.
priority_reason: Rider unreliability combined with lazy CS resolution (auto-cancel) guarantees churn.
action_item: Create automated re-assignment protocol and trigger priority CS alert for riders who remain stationary/unreachable for >10 mins post-pickup.
key_insight: Cancelling a delayed order without asking the customer is interpreted as the platform avoiding responsibility.

author: Parul_J
review_text: I am using subscription model from last 3 months and never have been there a case when one week went smoothly without me spending precious minutes of early morning in trying to resolve the issue. Customer care is super slow. Sometimes feel like they are still operating on registers and need to check details from physical copies. It takes that much of a time to respond. Accounting systems are messed up. Unnecessary added layer of locking money in subscription. Not considering the spent money in subscription model as part of spending eligible for gifts. It’s like nobody is putting in effort to think through the process while making decisions and work on the loop holes of the superficial decisions taken. Not at all smooth when compared to blinkit. The only pros left are premium products and first club prop products like juices bowls etc. if anyone else replicates, I’ll be happy to move on. Work on your pain points otherwise all the money burnt to create stickiness will be a waste.
sentiment_reason: User faces weekly subscription billing issues, extremely slow CS, and locked funds not counting towards loyalty gifts.
specific_issue: Subscription locks funds unnecessarily, CS is slow, and subscription spend doesn't count towards eligible gifts.
churn_reason: Weekly frustration with locked money and unresponsive support.
priority_reason: Financial trust break due to subscription billing issues combined with high churn risk.
action_item: Audit subscription billing chron-jobs for lock errors and patch the loyalty points logic to include subscription spend.
key_insight: Broken subscription billing and exclusion from loyalty programs are penalizing our most predictable revenue segment.

author: Vishalaakshi
review_text: So, apart from vegetables and a few perishable items like buttermilk, it looks like their diligence has left the chat. I was so happy knowing there that finally, we have a platform that is better than the others. But unfortunately, they are slowly turning towards the same direction. Ordered a fruit and it had small worms which was difficult to catch. An expired packet of paneer was delivered which fortunately caught my eyes while checking the price. Would have never deliberately  checked the expiry due to the trust that I had on the brand. And to top it all, the rice I ordered, the grains in it were sticking with each other indicating an infestation. Again, not everything happened in one go. The vegetables I believe are fresh (can’t guarantee) but rest of it, you all carefully check before consuming.
sentiment_reason: Severe product quality degradation including worms in fruit, expired paneer, and bugs in rice.
specific_issue: Delivered worms in fruit, expired paneer, and insect-infested rice.
churn_reason: Loss of trust in food safety and quality control.
priority_reason: Major safety/quality trust break; needs operational and technical safeguards.
action_item: Implement a mandatory barcode expiry scan in the packer app before dispatching perishable SKUs.
key_insight: Critical lapses in warehouse QA (expired items, bugs) are instantly destroying the premium brand trust.

sentiment_reason: Trusts the quality enough to select without worry.

key_insight: Failing to honor promotional freebies is perceived as deceitful and severely damages the premium brand promise.

author: suj92
review_text:
I’ve recently started using the FirstClub app for my grocery and daily essentials, and it’s honestly been a refreshing experience. What really stands out is their commitment to offering products without artificial colours or added sugar and it makes shopping feel much more thoughtful and health-conscious.

The app is easy to navigate, and I like how curated everything feels compared to typical grocery platforms. It doesn’t overwhelm you with endless options, but instead focuses on quality choices.

Delivery has been smooth so far, and the overall experience feels reliable. If you’re someone who cares about clean, healthier food options for your family, FirstClub is definitely worth trying.

priority_reason: Excellent validation of the brand's core value proposition.
key_insight: A limited, highly curated catalog reduces overwhelm and feels premium compared to typical endless-aisle platforms.

author: Pikachuu190

specific_issue: App crashes or fails to load when clicking certain category pages.
churn_reason: Bugs preventing category browsing directly block purchases.
priority_reason: Actionable, revenue-blocking bug in app routing.

author: Anki3092
review_text: Excellent UI and product range
sentiment_reason: Praises excellent UI and product range.

author: poor bank
review_text:
The delivery staff should receive proper training on hygiene. Today, while handing over my order, the delivery person kept other delivery bags on the ground very close to dirty slippers and an unhygienic area. When I pointed it out and asked why he was doing that, he replied that it was not my bag and it was for other deliveries.

This kind of handling is not acceptable, especially when it involves food or consumable products. Even if the bag is meant for another customer, it should still be handled in a clean and hygienic way. Please ensure that delivery staff are trained to maintain proper hygiene during the delivery process.
sentiment_reason: Disgusted by the delivery partner keeping other delivery bags on the unhygienic ground.
specific_issue: Delivery staff place grocery bags on dirty floors during drop-offs.
churn_reason: Poor hygiene at the last mile ruins the premium and healthy brand image.
priority_reason: Operational/training issue that requires a tech intervention for compliance.
action_item: Add a mandatory hygiene checklist acknowledgement step in the delivery partner app before starting a trip.
key_insight: Last-mile handling hygiene is highly visible to customers and can instantly undo upstream quality efforts.

author: _Arunav_
review_text: Nowadays most quick delivery apps focus so much on speed that the quality of fruits and vegetables often gets ignored. What I like about FirstClub is that they still focus on quality. The fruits and vegetables are fresh and well selected, and the quality has been consistent. It gives peace of mind because you can place an order without worrying about what will arrive. Honestly, this kind of reliability was really needed.
sentiment_reason: Values the consistent quality of fresh fruits and vegetables over sheer speed.
priority_reason: Strong validation of prioritizing quality over extreme speed.
key_insight: Users will trade 10-minute delivery for guaranteed produce quality and reliability.

author: Ritu68

author: Hemu Jake
review_text:
I had a very disappointing experience with FirstClub. I cleared the first round of the interview and was told there would be a second round, but there has been no proper communication or scheduling after that. The recruitment process seems very unorganized and unprofessional.

Candidates invest their time preparing for interviews, so the company should at least provide clear updates or feedback. Ignoring candidates after the first round reflects poorly on the company’s management and hiring process. Very disappointing experience.
sentiment_reason: Complaining about the company's unorganized HR/recruiting process after an interview.
specific_issue: No feedback or scheduling after clearing the first round of a job interview.
priority_reason: HR issue posted on the app store; not a product defect.
key_insight: Candidates are using the app store to vent about internal recruiting processes.

author: Ajaydobliyal
review_text: This is really amazing app. I love to share this feedback. They have really done an amazing job.

author: App glitche
review_text: Too many glitches in the app, moreover it doesn’t allow to edit the subscription frequency, no one time delivery, the only option is subscription or delivery charges for quick delivery.
sentiment_reason: Too many app glitches, cannot edit subscription frequency, and annoyed by lack of one-time delivery option.
specific_issue: Cannot edit subscription frequency and no option for one-time delivery without heavy fees.
feature_request: Allow editing of subscription frequency and enable standard one-time deliveries.
churn_reason: Subscription rigidity and app glitches create high friction for ordering.
priority_reason: Actionable product gaps in the subscription management flow that block revenue.
action_item: Implement UI and backend endpoints to allow users to edit subscription frequencies, and surface a one-time purchase toggle.
key_insight: Rigid subscription mechanics without edit capabilities actively frustrate and deter users.

author: Advin Netto
review_text: I’m a sucker for quality, and First Club is delivering it in spades. The delivery experience is smooth, and as a designer, I deeply appreciate the attention to detail in the branding (the bags and delivery guy’s shirts look great!). The only downside is the limited selection beyond food. When I need to bundle orders with household items like batteries or medicine, I’m forced to use other apps. I’d love to see this expand into a one stop shop for high quality essentials! Thanks for bringing this app to us! ❤️
sentiment_reason: Loves the quality and delivery, but frustrated by the lack of household essentials, forcing use of other apps.
specific_issue: Limited catalog outside of food items (e.g., batteries, medicine).
feature_request: Expand catalog into non-food household essentials.
priority_reason: Business strategy/catalog request rather than an engineering fix.
key_insight: The strict food curation prevents users from consolidating their entire grocery list on the app.

author: Piyushkv210
review_text:
Comparing to other apps, here we are getting transparency on products. For example adding certificate for organic produce is good. Few suggestions: 
For unbranded products like vegetables, if you are showing 2 types and one is tagged organic with certificate and another is not but priced almost one third, it creates doubt and confusion that if the cheaper product without any certification can trusted?? 


Please add more transparency layers like pesticide free, chemical free. 

Add some education layer around how to know that what to check in these kind of products
sentiment_reason: User appreciates organic certificates but feels confused about uncertified products and wants more transparency.
specific_issue: Discrepancy and confusion between certified organic and cheaper uncertified products.
feature_request: Add transparency layers (pesticide/chemical free tags) and an educational layer.
priority_reason: Clear feature request for UX and product metadata enhancement.
action_item: Implement visual tags (e.g., 'pesticide-free') and tooltip explanations for uncertified vs certified produce.
key_insight: Price gaps between certified and uncertified items create trust friction if not properly contextualized.

author: Akhil2807
review_text:
The app looks really clean but dig a little deeper, you find quite a lot of features not functioning properly. Product discovery is messy. There are a lot of products they offer but really hard to find them. They show a lot of cool banner showcasing brands, but nothing happens when you tap on them. Some products are not categorized properly and the search is finicky. It’s almost like you just need to know exactly what you want if you have to order from here.

author: e311993
review_text:
I noticed what appears to be a very concerning and misleading pricing practice by FirstClub.

On the app, strawberries are listed at ₹145 for 160–200 grams. I ordered two boxes expecting the quantity to fall within that range. However, one box weighed 200 grams while the other was only 160 grams, yet both were charged the same price of ₹145. I do not understand how a 40-gram difference in product can be justified at the same price.

On closer inspection, the issue is even more troubling. In the box labeled as 160 grams, the actual strawberries weighed only 150 grams, while the packaging itself weighed 12 grams. This effectively means customers are paying for the box as if it were part of the fruit weight.

Attaching images for reference.

If the listed weight includes packaging, that should be clearly disclosed. Otherwise, this feels misleading—customers are paying for fresh fruit but receiving less product than advertised. I would appreciate clarity and accountability on this matter.
sentiment_reason: User discovered misleading pricing where product weight was under the listed range and included packaging weight.
specific_issue: Strawberries sold as 160-200g actually contained 150g of product, with packaging making up the rest, despite premium pricing.
churn_reason: User explicitly calls it a 'concerning and misleading pricing practice'.
priority_reason: Deceptive measurement practices severely break trust regarding value/money.
action_item: Audit vendor weight compliance for fresh produce to ensure net weight (excluding packaging) meets listed minimums.
key_insight: Including packaging in the listed net weight is perceived as fraudulent by premium customers.

review_text: I ordered 17 items from bangalore team and they sent me 14 items- promised some dry fruits hamper as Dey launched it today- when delivery boy came , I kept it without checking as I was busy in household work-after half hour when I checked - costly sardough bread was missing , green capsicum/yellow capsicum/ red capsicum was missing which implies that they are stealing money from customers from day one bang on to make profits from middle class families like mine who are a trying to make ends meet in a costly city like bangalore , otherwise why would they choose the costly items to be kept back in the warehouse ??? Not sure - most mods spoiler they sent free dry fruits hamper to everyone in my apartment other than me - so this comes under racial discrimination act - I don’t know what more policies they are having - I was the first target rather victim - we don’t know how many of you will be affected - so stay alert of companies like this because it’s our hard earned money but BAU for them means business as usual for them to empty the pockets of middle class families like us - already we have to give GST and now this king of lapses and corruption and racism in the system - THIS IS A COMPLETE NO FOR ALL MIDDLE CLASS FAMILIES IN BANGALORE- don’t get trapped by their calls , free hampers - ZEPTO , INSTAMART, BIGBASKET and BLINKIT are far far better than this company. Last but not the least - STAY SAFE , STAY ALERT

specific_issue: OTP invalid when trying to delete account; applying referral code requires extra steps.

author: Raviraj Mangalpady
review_text:
A Hidden Gem for Clean, Premium Groceries

I genuinely hope FirstClub continues to grow because it is one of the best-kept secrets in grocery shopping right now. For some reason, they don’t market themselves very aggressively, but their products completely speak for themselves.

Quality & Pricing
If you are looking for truly clean groceries, fresh fruits, vegetables, and guilt-free snacks, FirstClub is fantastic. What stands out just as much as their product purity is their pricing. The costs are
on par with Lulu Hypermarket. Considering I’ve always ranked Lulu as the second-best when it comes to quality, finding a brand that matches those prices while offering such clean, top-tier products is a massive win.

The Verdict
Overall, FirstClub is highly recommended. It’s rare to find a brand that balances affordability, incredibly clean products, and eco-conscious packaging so well. Give them a try—they deserve the hype!
sentiment_reason: Customer is thrilled with the premium quality, clean groceries, and competitive pricing.
priority_reason: Positive review emphasizing value for money against competitors.
key_insight: Pricing is perceived favorably when compared directly to premium hypermarket competitors.

themes: Product Quality | Returns & Refunds | Customer Support

review_text: This company seems to fool people. I saw their ad on insta and wanted to try out. I bought mangos and paid Rs 220 thinking that it's a 1kg mango. That's my mistake. So when I looked at my cart before ordering, I saw two free items -  idli batter and a packet of dry fruits. It was s pleasant surprise. When the order arrived, it was another surprise. A nicely packed small mango around 100g and another big marketing bag to carry it. The free items that was shown to me was just a bait. I cut the mango and it was a rotten mango. See the attached picture. I called the customer service and someone picked up the call and that was a surprise. I got a refund of Rs 199! They took the rest, but that's okay.  They claim the products are certified organic but no such marking on the box or where they got certified. Maybe certified by the delivery agent or some employee of the company. A great way to fool people.
sentiment_reason: Customer feels bait-and-switched by confusing unit weights, missing cart freebies, and receiving rotten fruit.
specific_issue: Misleading mango weight (assumed 1kg, got 100g), missing free items shown in cart, and the 100g mango delivered was rotten.
churn_reason: Customer explicitly claims the company 'fools people' and uses bait tactics.
priority_reason: Trust broken on three fronts: unit pricing clarity, missing promotional items, and rotten product.
action_item: Force display of unit weights in bold on cart/checkout and ensure cart promo items map strictly to fulfillment packing lists.
key_insight: Ambiguous unit pricing combined with unfulfilled cart freebies creates a severe perception of fraud.

author: rashmi gowrishankar
review_text: I have subscription the product said delivered and not delivered. This is second time and all fall promise
sentiment_reason: Customer's subscription orders are being marked delivered but not actually arriving.
specific_issue: Subscription items marked as delivered but missing; this is a repeat offense.
churn_reason: Repeat failure of paid subscription delivery creates massive frustration ('all false promise').
priority_reason: Customer is charged for subscription drops they are not receiving.
action_item: Implement mandatory delivery photo proof or OTP for subscription drop-offs to prevent false delivery statuses.
key_insight: False delivery markers on recurring subscriptions are immediate trust-breakers requiring strict proof-of-delivery gates.

author: V M
review_text: When will pincode 560043 servicable?
sentiment_reason: Customer is simply inquiring about service availability in their area.
specific_issue: Pincode 560043 is currently not serviceable.
feature_request: Add serviceability for pincode 560043.
priority_reason: Actionable growth signal, but not an urgent operational fire.
action_item: Add 'Notify me when available' lead capture for unserviceable pincodes to build a geographic expansion backlog.
key_insight: Unserviceable queries represent latent demand that should be captured systematically.

author: Amrutha Sujith
review_text: Im glad that they are providing high quality produces. All my purchases were good and I hope they maintain the quality.
sentiment_reason: Customer is satisfied with high quality produce.
key_insight: High quality produce remains the core retention driver.

author: Freesoul Colive
review_text: This is the worst and don't believe as it was the horrible personal experience. I ordered carrot juice which labelled as fresh and no preservatives but I'm reality nothing fresh and they just added colour to the carrot juice.adn and I'm accusing and making false allegations here this is my genuine and experienced review and I'm the victim here I have the right tosay the truth and I strongly say this because it's 💯 true
sentiment_reason: Customer feels deceived, alleging that artificial color was added to 'fresh' carrot juice.
specific_issue: Carrot juice labeled as fresh/no preservatives appears to contain artificial color, breaking product claims.
churn_reason: Customer feels deeply deceived and is adamant about exposing the 'truth'.
priority_reason: Severe allegation of product misrepresentation; while not a direct safety threat, it shatters the 'clean' brand promise.
action_item: Publish lab test results or ingredient transparency certificates directly on juice product detail pages to dispel artificial color claims.
key_insight: When a brand's core promise is 'clean' food, any perceived adulteration causes a catastrophic loss of trust.

author: rahul shivanand
review_text: Stop bombarding so many ads,. It's really annoying
sentiment_reason: Customer is extremely annoyed by ad frequency.
specific_issue: Being bombarded with too many advertisements.
churn_reason: Ad fatigue can lead to app uninstalls or notification muting.
priority_reason: Growth/marketing friction point, easy to resolve.
action_item: Implement frequency capping for push notifications and retargeting ads per user ID.
key_insight: Over-marketing is creating negative brand equity among potential or existing users.

author: RAMAKRISHNA REDDY KUNTLA
review_text: Very Poor Quality. We have subscribed for Tender coconut daily. The first order its was bad. The Water is cloudy and smelling very bad. Its just not with me. Most of the residents in our apartment also had similar experience. There was also a price increase without our knowledge from 55 to 67 a piece.
sentiment_reason: Customer received bad coconuts on a subscription and was subjected to a silent price hike.
specific_issue: Cloudy, bad-smelling tender coconut water on subscription, plus an unannounced price increase from 55 to 67.
churn_reason: Combination of spoiled goods and an unauthorized price hike on recurring billing.
priority_reason: Silent price increases on subscriptions combined with bad quality represent a critical trust and billing violation.
action_item: Add explicit push/email confirmation flows for any price changes on active subscriptions before the next billing cycle.
key_insight: Altering subscription prices without explicit user opt-in is viewed as predatory and causes immediate churn.

author: Ramesh Babu
review_text: Worst organisation, twice in an order green one green apple missed each time , when i raise complaint they said they wil refund the money for green apple I didn't accept money as , in a new platform if inside org they miss item( it is not during delivery) inside the ware house only it is missing , they couldnt even act on missing item til now , I didn't accept refund , if they cannot keep happy with a customer who complain what nonsense it is and no courtesy call they did to a customer who place order daily , I have decided not to use this until I get a call from them
sentiment_reason: Customer is upset over a missing apple, refused a refund, and expects a courtesy call from the warehouse.
specific_issue: Missing green apple twice; support offered a refund but customer refused, demanding a courtesy call instead.
priority_reason: Customer expectation (refusing refund and demanding warehouse call for one apple) is highly unrealistic.
key_insight: While fulfillment accuracy matters, refusing valid refunds to demand personalized apologies cannot scale.

author: Disha Oberoi
review_text:
What a find.
I’ve been using First Club for a few months now. No one has attained and maintained this level of quality with fresh produce in particular.

The economically priced fresh juices are a big plus. Delivery time - as much as any quick delivery platform. Must try!
sentiment_reason: Customer praises the maintained quality and economical fresh juices.
priority_reason: Strong positive review validating price-to-quality ratio.
key_insight: Fresh juices are a strong anchor product combining quality perception with value.

author: Rishika Boinapally
review_text: The platform only has brands which are lab tested and non toxic. Impressed with the concept. Received great quality fruits. In love with the app
sentiment_reason: Customer appreciates the focus on lab-tested, non-toxic brands.
priority_reason: Positive reinforcement of the curation strategy.
key_insight: Curating exclusively non-toxic/lab-tested brands is a massive differentiator.
